articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh' to Bolster Agricultural Resilience and Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh', a new fund aimed at strengthening the agricultural sector.
• The Kosh will focus on providing financial support for climate-resilient agriculture, modern irrigation techniques, and post-harvest management.
• It seeks to enhance farmers' income by promoting value addition, market linkages, and reducing agricultural waste.
• Special emphasis will be given to small and marginal farmers, with provisions for easier access to credit and technology.
• The initiative aligns with the government's broader vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and doubling farmers' income.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage
Transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Name of the new fund: Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh.
Key objectives: Climate-resilient agriculture, modern irrigation, post-harvest management, farmer income enhancement.
Beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers.
Alignment with: Atmanirbhar Bharat, Doubling Farmers' Income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh in addressing contemporary challenges faced by the Indian agricultural sector, such as climate change impacts and market inefficiencies.
Analyze how the fund's focus on technology and market linkages can contribute to the long-term sustainability and profitability of farming.
Critically evaluate the potential impact on small and marginal farmers and the broader rural economy.
Examine the role of such targeted funds in achieving the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in the agricultural domain.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a farmer in a lush field with modern farming equipment and a government scheme logo.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, cotton, and groundnuts.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Privacy in Digital Healthcare Records, Mandates Robust Data Protection Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, affirmed that the right to privacy extends to an individual's digital healthcare data.
• The ruling mandates the government to establish a comprehensive data protection framework specifically for health records, ensuring consent-based access and strict confidentiality.
• The court emphasized the need for robust security measures to prevent data breaches and misuse of sensitive personal health information.
• This judgment comes in the context of increasing digitization of health services and the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
• It highlights the delicate balance between technological advancement in healthcare and safeguarding fundamental rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on Right to Privacy regarding digital healthcare data.
Mandates government to establish data protection framework for health records.
Emphasizes consent-based access and confidentiality.
Context: Digitization of health services, Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the ongoing digitization of healthcare services in India, particularly for initiatives like the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust data protection framework for sensitive health information, balancing innovation with individual rights.
Examine the constitutional basis for the right to privacy in the digital age and its evolving interpretation by the judiciary.
Suggest measures the government can take to ensure compliance with the ruling and build public trust in digital health platforms.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel on a legal document with a digital medical icon in the background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Enhanced Maritime Security and Trade Facilitation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing deeper cooperation in maritime security, trade, and digital connectivity.
• Leaders agreed on a roadmap to counter traditional and non-traditional maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Discussions also focused on expediting negotiations for the review of the ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) to boost bilateral trade.
• India reiterated its commitment to ASEAN's centrality in the Indo-Pacific region and the importance of a rules-based international order.
• Digital transformation and green economy initiatives were identified as new pillars for future collaboration.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Focus areas of India-ASEAN Summit: Maritime security, trade, digital connectivity.
Agreement to counter piracy, illegal fishing, drug trafficking.
Review of ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA).
India's stance on ASEAN centrality in Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of enhanced maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss how the proposed review of AITIGA can benefit India's economic ties with Southeast Asian nations and contribute to regional economic integration.
Examine India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in strengthening relations with ASEAN, highlighting challenges and opportunities.
Evaluate the potential for India-ASEAN collaboration in emerging areas like digital transformation and green economy initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 by Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

articles.headline: New National Green Hydrogen Mission Guidelines Released, Focus on Production Incentives and R&D
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released detailed guidelines for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, outlining implementation strategies.
• The guidelines include a scheme for incentivizing green hydrogen production and promoting domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• A significant portion of the mission's outlay is earmarked for research and development (R&D) in cost-effective green hydrogen technologies.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing dependence on fossil fuels.
• It also emphasizes skill development and creating a robust regulatory framework for the burgeoning green hydrogen ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
Key components: Production incentives, electrolyzer manufacturing, R&D.
Objective: India as a global green hydrogen hub, fossil fuel reduction.
Other focus areas: Skill development, regulatory framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the economic implications of incentivizing green hydrogen production and domestic electrolyzer manufacturing on India's industrial sector.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into the existing energy infrastructure.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen as a key driver for decarbonization across various sectors of the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity through a process called electrolysis.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype, Paving Way for Cost-Effective Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of the vehicle from space, demonstrating autonomous navigation and precision landing capabilities.
• This achievement is a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space mission costs.
• The RLV technology is critical for future human spaceflight missions (Gaganyaan) and deploying larger satellite constellations.
• ISRO aims to achieve space transportation at a fraction of current costs, enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's successful test: Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
Nature of test: Controlled landing experiment.
Objective: Reduce space mission costs, develop two-stage orbital vehicle.
Significance: Future human spaceflight (Gaganyaan), satellite deployment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its aspirations in the global space market.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable for various applications, including scientific research and commercial launches.
Examine the technical challenges involved in developing and perfecting RLV technology, drawing parallels with global efforts in this domain.
Evaluate the potential impact of this achievement on India's human spaceflight mission (Gaganyaan) and its long-term space exploration goals.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a winged body aerospace vehicle operating in hypersonic flight regime.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI noted a moderation in core inflation but highlighted risks from volatile food prices and geopolitical tensions.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while remaining agile in its policy stance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is likely to maintain a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance for the foreseeable future.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5%.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision.
Reason: Balance inflation with growth, global uncertainties.
Stance: 'Withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst evolving domestic and global economic conditions.
Analyze the implications of the RBI's monetary policy stance on inflation, economic growth, and the financial sector in India.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation, particularly considering factors like food price volatility and global supply chain disruptions.
Critically evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its potential impact on credit growth and investment.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance Along Western Border with AI-Powered Drones and Integrated Command System
articles.explainer:
• India's Border Security Forces (BSF) have significantly upgraded their surveillance capabilities along the western border.
• The deployment includes advanced AI-powered drones equipped with thermal imaging and facial recognition technology.
• These drones are integrated into a centralized command and control system, enabling real-time threat assessment and rapid response.
• The initiative aims to counter infiltration attempts, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism more effectively.
• This technological leap is part of a broader strategy to modernize India's border management and enhance national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: BSF's new technology: AI-powered drones.
Location: Western border.
Features: Thermal imaging, facial recognition, integrated command system.
Purpose: Counter infiltration, smuggling, terrorism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of technology, specifically AI-powered drones, in modernizing border management and enhancing India's internal security architecture.
Analyze the advantages and challenges associated with deploying advanced surveillance technologies in sensitive border areas.
Examine how such technological advancements can deter cross-border infiltration and organized crime, while also considering ethical implications.
Suggest other measures that can complement technological solutions for comprehensive border security management in India.
articles.imageDescription: Drone flying over a desert landscape near a border fence at sunset.
articles.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is India's primary border guarding organization on its border with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of technology in border security

articles.headline: National Health Mission Launches 'POSHAN 2.0' Initiative to Combat Malnutrition with Integrated Approach
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) officially launched 'POSHAN 2.0', an upgraded and integrated national nutrition mission.
• The initiative aims to address malnutrition among children, adolescent girls, pregnant women, and lactating mothers through a multi-sectoral approach.
• Key features include enhanced focus on early childhood care, promotion of traditional food systems, and leveraging technology for monitoring.
• It integrates the erstwhile Supplementary Nutrition Programme and the POSHAN Abhiyaan, streamlining efforts for greater impact.
• POSHAN 2.0 emphasizes community participation and behavioral change communication to achieve its targets.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Initiative: POSHAN 2.0.
Launched by: National Health Mission (NHM).
Target beneficiaries: Children, adolescent girls, pregnant & lactating mothers.
Approach: Multi-sectoral, integrates previous schemes, leverages technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and objectives of POSHAN 2.0 and how it seeks to improve upon its predecessor, POSHAN Abhiyaan, in addressing malnutrition in India.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach adopted by POSHAN 2.0 and its potential effectiveness in tackling the complex issue of malnutrition.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in the successful implementation of such large-scale nutrition programs.
Critically evaluate the challenges that POSHAN 2.0 might face in achieving its targets and suggest measures to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing healthy children and diverse food, with POSHAN 2.0 logo.
articles.trivia: The original POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government schemes for health and nutrition

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Political Stability and Strengthen Democracy
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution (Anti-Defection Law).
• Key recommendations include limiting the Speaker's discretionary powers in defection cases and setting a strict timeline for decisions.
• The committee also suggested clarifying what constitutes 'voluntary giving up of membership' and 'defiance of party whip'.
• The aim is to curb frequent defections, enhance political stability, and preserve the integrity of the democratic process.
• The report advocates for a clearer definition of 'split' and 'merger' to prevent misuse of the law.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Recommendations by: Parliamentary Standing Committee.
Subject: Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law).
Key recommendations: Limit Speaker's power, strict timeline, clarify 'voluntary giving up' and 'defiance of whip'.
Objective: Curb defections, enhance stability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and objectives of the Anti-Defection Law and identify its shortcomings that necessitate reforms.
Analyze the specific recommendations made by the parliamentary committee, particularly regarding the Speaker's role and the definition of defection events.
Examine how these proposed reforms could impact political stability, party discipline, and the functioning of parliamentary democracy in India.
Critically evaluate the balance between ensuring party loyalty and protecting the freedom of expression of individual legislators under the anti-defection framework.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a legal document overlay.
articles.trivia: The Anti-Defection Law was added to the Constitution in 1985 by the 52nd Amendment Act.

articles.headline: IMD Issues Early Warning for Cyclonic Storm in Bay of Bengal, Preparedness Measures Underway
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an early warning for a potential cyclonic storm forming in the Bay of Bengal.
• Coastal regions of Odisha, West Bengal, and Andhra Pradesh have been put on high alert, with fishermen advised to return to shore.
• National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) teams have been pre-positioned, and state disaster management authorities are coordinating evacuation plans.
• The warning emphasizes the importance of timely information dissemination and community preparedness in mitigating the impact of natural disasters.
• IMD is continuously monitoring the system's development and will issue further updates.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Warning by: IMD.
Event: Cyclonic storm in Bay of Bengal.
Affected states: Odisha, West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh.
Agencies involved: NDRF, state disaster management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of India's early warning systems and disaster preparedness mechanisms in mitigating the impact of cyclonic storms, citing recent examples.
Analyze the challenges faced by coastal communities and disaster management agencies during cyclonic events and suggest measures for improving resilience.
Examine the role of different stakeholders, including central and state governments, local communities, and NGOs, in comprehensive disaster management.
Evaluate the long-term strategies required to adapt to increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events due to climate change.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing a cyclonic formation over the Bay of Bengal.
articles.trivia: Tropical cyclones in the North Indian Ocean are named by countries in the region, following a specific list.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Progress, Focus on Services and Digital Trade
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have shown significant progress in key areas, including services and digital trade.
• Both sides expressed optimism about reaching a mutually beneficial agreement that could unlock substantial economic opportunities.
• Key sticking points remain on issues like market access for certain agricultural products and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, reduce tariffs, and harmonize regulatory standards.
• A strong India-EU trade pact is seen as crucial for diversifying global supply chains and strengthening strategic autonomy for both partners.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Negotiations for: India-EU FTA.
Progress in: Services and digital trade.
Sticking points: Agricultural market access, IPR.
Objectives: Boost trade, reduce tariffs, harmonize standards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy, particularly its services and manufacturing sectors, from an FTA with the EU.
Examine the key areas of contention in the negotiations, such as agricultural market access and intellectual property rights, and how they can be resolved.
Evaluate the broader geopolitical implications of a strong India-EU trade partnership in the context of global supply chain diversification and multilateral trade dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwining.
articles.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Discover Novel Enzyme for Plastic Degradation, Offering Hope for Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) have identified a novel enzyme capable of efficiently degrading polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic.
• This enzyme, discovered in a local bacterial strain, shows promising results in breaking down plastic at ambient temperatures, unlike many existing solutions.
• The discovery holds significant potential for developing scalable and eco-friendly methods for plastic waste management and recycling.
• Further research is underway to optimize the enzyme's activity and explore its application in industrial settings.
• This breakthrough could contribute significantly to addressing India's growing plastic pollution crisis.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Environmental pollution and degradation
Bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: Indian Institute of Science (IISc) researchers.
Discovery: Novel enzyme for PET plastic degradation.
Key feature: Efficient at ambient temperatures.
Potential: Scalable, eco-friendly plastic recycling.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of discovering a novel enzyme for plastic degradation in addressing the global plastic pollution crisis, particularly in the Indian context.
Analyze the advantages of enzymatic degradation methods over traditional plastic recycling techniques in terms of environmental impact and efficiency.
Examine the challenges involved in scaling up such biotechnological solutions for industrial application and their potential economic viability.
Suggest how this scientific breakthrough can be integrated into India's broader waste management strategies and contribute to a circular economy.
articles.imageDescription: Microscope image of bacteria on plastic, with an enzyme model overlay.
articles.trivia: PET plastic is commonly used for beverage bottles and food containers.

articles.headline: Actor Saif Ali Khan Stabbed During Alleged Robbery Attempt at Mumbai Residence
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Bollywood actor Saif Ali Khan was reportedly stabbed multiple times during an attempted robbery at his Mumbai residence.
• Police launched an immediate investigation, with initial reports suggesting the involvement of an alleged illegal Bangladeshi national.
• The incident has reignited concerns about urban crime rates and the challenges of managing illegal immigration in metropolitan areas.
• Security measures for celebrities and high-profile individuals in Mumbai are under review following the high-profile attack.
• The actor is reported to be in stable condition after receiving medical attention.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
articles.imageDescription: Police tape outside a residential building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: Mumbai is often referred to as the 'Financial Capital of India'.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Odisha.
• The excavation site, located near the Udayagiri-Ratnagiri-Lalitgiri complex, has yielded stupas, prayer halls, and monastic cells.
• Artifacts such as Buddha statues, terracotta seals, and pottery dating back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods have been found.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of Odisha.
• The site is expected to become a major archaeological and tourist attraction, bolstering cultural tourism in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: ASI.
Discovery: Ancient Buddhist monastery remains.
Location: Odisha, near Udayagiri-Ratnagiri-Lalitgiri complex.
Artifacts: Stupas, Buddha statues, terracotta seals, pottery (Gupta/post-Gupta).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the discovery of ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Odisha for understanding the historical spread and development of Buddhism in Eastern India.
Analyze how such archaeological findings contribute to the broader narrative of India's cultural heritage and its connections with other ancient civilizations.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting newly discovered archaeological sites, particularly in terms of tourism and local development.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in exploring and protecting India's rich historical legacy.
articles.imageDescription: Excavation site showing ancient brick structures and a Buddha statue.
articles.trivia: The Udayagiri, Ratnagiri, and Lalitgiri hills in Odisha form the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites.

articles.headline: National Education Policy Implementation: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced new initiatives under the National Education Policy (NEP) to integrate vocational training into higher education.
• The focus is on making graduates industry-ready by offering skill-based courses, apprenticeships, and certifications alongside traditional degrees.
• This move aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce and enhance the employability of Indian youth.
• Collaborations with industries and Sector Skill Councils are being formalized to design relevant curricula and provide practical exposure.
• The policy emphasizes a multidisciplinary approach, allowing students to combine academic learning with vocational skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Initiative under: National Education Policy (NEP).
Focus: Vocational training in higher education.
Methods: Skill-based courses, apprenticeships, industry collaboration.
Objectives: Bridge skill gap, enhance employability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind integrating vocational training into higher education as envisioned by the National Education Policy 2020, and its potential impact on India's workforce.
Analyze how such reforms can address the skill gap and unemployment challenges among Indian youth, particularly in a rapidly evolving job market.
Examine the role of industry-academia collaboration and Sector Skill Councils in successfully implementing vocational education programs.
Critically evaluate the challenges in transitioning from a traditional academic-focused system to a more multidisciplinary and skill-oriented higher education framework.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a vocational training workshop, learning practical skills.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, making it the first new education policy in 34 years.

articles.headline: India Crosses 100 GW Mark in Installed Solar Power Capacity, Boosting Renewable Energy Targets
articles.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone by crossing 100 Gigawatts (GW) of installed solar power capacity.
• This includes both large-scale solar parks and rooftop solar installations across the country.
• The achievement underscores India's rapid progress in the renewable energy sector and its commitment to climate goals.
• This milestone significantly contributes to India's target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Government policies, subsidies, and private sector investments have been instrumental in driving this growth.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Milestone: 100 GW installed solar power capacity.
Includes: Solar parks and rooftop solar.
Contribution to: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy target by 2030.
Drivers: Government policies, subsidies, private investment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India crossing 100 GW in solar power capacity for its energy security, climate commitments, and economic development.
Analyze the key policy measures and initiatives that have facilitated the rapid growth of solar energy in India.
Examine the challenges that India faces in further scaling up its renewable energy capacity, particularly in terms of grid integration, land acquisition, and storage solutions.
Evaluate the role of solar power in achieving India's broader target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030 and its implications for global climate action.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panel field under a bright sun.
articles.trivia: The world's largest solar park, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

articles.headline: Geological Survey of India Confirms New Lithium Reserves in Rajasthan, Boosting EV Ambitions
articles.explainer:
• The Geological Survey of India (GSI) confirmed the discovery of significant lithium reserves in the Degana region of Rajasthan.
• This discovery follows a similar finding in Jammu and Kashmir, further enhancing India's potential for domestic lithium production.
• Lithium is a critical component for electric vehicle (EV) batteries and various high-tech industries, making this a strategic find.
• Domestic sourcing of lithium is crucial for India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the EV sector and reducing import dependence.
• The government plans to fast-track exploration and extraction, adhering to environmental sustainability norms.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Mineral resources
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: Geological Survey of India (GSI).
Discovery: Significant lithium reserves.
Location: Degana region, Rajasthan (after J&K).
Significance: Critical for EV batteries, 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of domestic lithium discoveries for India's electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing sector and its broader energy transition goals.
Analyze the economic implications of reducing import dependence on critical minerals like lithium for India's industrial growth and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Examine the challenges associated with the exploration, extraction, and processing of lithium, particularly in ensuring environmental sustainability and local community engagement.
Suggest a roadmap for India to leverage these new discoveries to become a significant player in the global EV battery supply chain.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of lithium-ion battery cells and a map of Rajasthan.
articles.trivia: Lithium is the lightest metal and the least dense solid element.

articles.headline: India Records Record Growth in Digital Payments, UPI Transactions Soar
articles.explainer:
• India's digital payments ecosystem continued its robust growth, with Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions reaching new highs.
• The latest data from the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) shows a significant increase in both volume and value of transactions.
• This surge is attributed to increased digital literacy, government initiatives promoting cashless transactions, and widespread merchant adoption.
• The growth in digital payments is a key indicator of financial inclusion and formalization of the economy.
• UPI's interoperability and user-friendly interface have made it a global benchmark for instant payment systems.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Record growth in: Digital payments, particularly UPI.
Data from: NPCI.
Reasons for growth: Digital literacy, government initiatives, merchant adoption.
Impact: Financial inclusion, formalization of economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the exponential growth of digital payments, especially UPI, in India and its implications for financial inclusion.
Analyze how a robust digital payment infrastructure supports the formalization of the economy and aids in revenue collection for the government.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with further expanding digital payment adoption in rural and semi-urban areas of India.
Evaluate UPI's role as a global benchmark for instant payment systems and India's potential to export its digital payment model to other developing nations.
articles.imageDescription: People making payments using smartphones with UPI logos.
articles.trivia: UPI was launched in 2016 by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).

articles.headline: Government Amends E-Waste Management Rules, Focus on Extended Producer Responsibility and Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, 2022.
• The revised rules place greater emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets and promote a circular economy for electronics.
• New provisions include stricter compliance mechanisms for manufacturers, producers, and recyclers to manage e-waste more effectively.
• The amendments aim to formalize the e-waste recycling sector, reduce illegal dumping, and recover valuable materials.
• This move is crucial for mitigating environmental pollution and conserving natural resources in a rapidly digitizing India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Amendment to: E-Waste Management Rules, 2022.
Ministry: Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Key focus: EPR targets, circular economy for electronics.
Objectives: Formalize recycling, reduce dumping, recover materials.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of robust E-Waste Management Rules in India, considering the rapid growth of electronic consumption and its environmental implications.
Analyze the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its effectiveness in promoting a circular economy model for electronic goods.
Examine the challenges in implementing e-waste management regulations, particularly in formalizing the informal recycling sector and ensuring compliance.
Suggest additional measures or technological interventions that could enhance India's e-waste management infrastructure and reduce environmental impact.
articles.imageDescription: Pile of electronic waste with a recycling symbol.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world.

highlights: Government launches 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh' to boost agricultural resilience and farmer income with focus on climate-resilient farming and market linkages.
Supreme Court upholds right to privacy for digital healthcare records, mandating a robust data protection framework for sensitive health information.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with a joint declaration on enhanced maritime security cooperation and trade facilitation, reviewing the AITIGA.
ISRO successfully tests its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, a critical step towards cost-effective space missions and Gaganyaan.
New National Green Hydrogen Mission guidelines released, aiming to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production through incentives and R&D.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to provide financial support for climate-resilient agriculture and post-harvest management.
2. It primarily targets large commercial farmers for mechanization.
3. The initiative aligns with the 'Doubling Farmers' Income' objective.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the Kosh focuses on climate-resilient agriculture, modern irrigation, and post-harvest management. Statement 2 is incorrect as special emphasis is given to small and marginal farmers. Statement 3 is correct as the initiative aligns with the government's broader vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and doubling farmers' income.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark repo rate in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is entrusted with the responsibility of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology being developed by ISRO:
1. RLV aims to significantly reduce the cost of space missions.
2. It is crucial for India's human spaceflight mission, Gaganyaan.
3. The technology primarily focuses on deploying small satellites into low Earth orbit.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. RLV technology is designed to drastically reduce space mission costs and is critical for future human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan. Statement 3 is partially correct but not the primary focus; while it can deploy satellites, its core aim is cost reduction for various missions, including larger satellite constellations and human spaceflight.

prelimsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission guidelines recently released aim to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production. Which ministry is responsible for releasing these guidelines?
options: (b) Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
(c) Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
(d) Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsMocks.explanation: The detailed guidelines for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, outlining implementation strategies and incentives, were released by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following archaeological sites in Odisha is NOT part of the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites in Odisha consists of Udayagiri, Ratnagiri, and Lalitgiri. Dhauli is another significant Buddhist site in Odisha, known for its rock edicts, but it is not typically included in the 'Diamond Triangle'.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of domestic lithium discoveries for India's electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing sector and its broader energy transition goals. What challenges might India face in leveraging these reserves, and how can they be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Mineral resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the significance of lithium for EVs and energy transition. Detail the strategic importance for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and reducing import dependence. Identify challenges such as environmental impact of mining, technological expertise for processing, infrastructure for extraction, and regulatory frameworks. Suggest solutions like sustainable mining practices, R&D in processing, international collaborations, and policy support.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment upholding the right to privacy in digital healthcare records for the ongoing digitization of healthcare services in India. What measures should the government undertake to ensure a robust data protection framework that balances innovation with individual rights?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Role of IT in governance.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the context of digital healthcare and the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission. Explain the implications of the SC ruling on data privacy, emphasizing its impact on patient trust and data security. Discuss the need for a comprehensive data protection framework (e.g., Data Protection Act principles). Suggest measures: clear consent mechanisms, robust encryption, independent regulatory body, penalties for breaches, public awareness campaigns, and ethical guidelines for AI use in health data.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the key features and objectives of the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh' and critically evaluate its potential to address the contemporary challenges faced by the Indian agricultural sector. What role can technology and community participation play in its successful implementation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country; Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage; Transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; E-technology in the aid of farmers; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by outlining the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh's objectives: climate resilience, post-harvest management, farmer income. Discuss how these address challenges like climate change vulnerability, market access issues, and waste. Evaluate its potential benefits, especially for small and marginal farmers. Highlight the role of technology (e-NAM, precision farming, data analytics) and community participation (FPOs, self-help groups) in ensuring effective implementation and last-mile delivery. Conclude with challenges and recommendations.

quickBytes.text: The 100th Amendment Act of the Indian Constitution relates to the Land Boundary Agreement between India and Bangladesh.
The 'Green Revolution' in India is primarily associated with the introduction of high-yielding varieties (HYVs) of wheat and rice.
The term 'Base Effect' in economics refers to the impact of the previous year's inflation rate on the calculation of current inflation.
The 'National Register of Citizens' (NRC) is a register containing names of all genuine Indian citizens.
India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched in 1975 by the Soviet Union.
The 'Exclusive Economic Zone' (EEZ) extends to 200 nautical miles from the baseline, where a state has special rights over exploration and use of marine resources.
The 'Khangchendzonga National Park' in Sikkim is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized for both natural and cultural significance.
The 'Fiscal Deficit' represents the total borrowings required by the government to meet its expenditure.
The 'Panchayat Extension to Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act, 1996' empowers Gram Sabhas in scheduled areas, particularly regarding natural resources.
The 'Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana' (PMJDY) aims for comprehensive financial inclusion for all households.

GS1.headline: IMD Issues Early Warning for Cyclonic Storm in Bay of Bengal, Preparedness Measures Underway
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an early warning for a potential cyclonic storm forming in the Bay of Bengal.
• Coastal regions of Odisha, West Bengal, and Andhra Pradesh have been put on high alert, with fishermen advised to return to shore.
• National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) teams have been pre-positioned, and state disaster management authorities are coordinating evacuation plans.
• The warning emphasizes the importance of timely information dissemination and community preparedness in mitigating the impact of natural disasters.
• IMD is continuously monitoring the system's development and will issue further updates.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Warning by: IMD.
Event: Cyclonic storm in Bay of Bengal.
Affected states: Odisha, West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh.
Agencies involved: NDRF, state disaster management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of India's early warning systems and disaster preparedness mechanisms in mitigating the impact of cyclonic storms, citing recent examples.
Analyze the challenges faced by coastal communities and disaster management agencies during cyclonic events and suggest measures for improving resilience.
Examine the role of different stakeholders, including central and state governments, local communities, and NGOs, in comprehensive disaster management.
Evaluate the long-term strategies required to adapt to increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events due to climate change.
GS1.imageDescription: Satellite image showing a cyclonic formation over the Bay of Bengal.
GS1.trivia: Tropical cyclones in the North Indian Ocean are named by countries in the region, following a specific list.

GS1.headline: Actor Saif Ali Khan Stabbed During Alleged Robbery Attempt at Mumbai Residence
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Bollywood actor Saif Ali Khan was reportedly stabbed multiple times during an attempted robbery at his Mumbai residence.
• Police launched an immediate investigation, with initial reports suggesting the involvement of an alleged illegal Bangladeshi national.
• The incident has reignited concerns about urban crime rates and the challenges of managing illegal immigration in metropolitan areas.
• Security measures for celebrities and high-profile individuals in Mumbai are under review following the high-profile attack.
• The actor is reported to be in stable condition after receiving medical attention.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
GS1.imageDescription: Police tape outside a residential building in Mumbai.
GS1.trivia: Mumbai is often referred to as the 'Financial Capital of India'.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Odisha.
• The excavation site, located near the Udayagiri-Ratnagiri-Lalitgiri complex, has yielded stupas, prayer halls, and monastic cells.
• Artifacts such as Buddha statues, terracotta seals, and pottery dating back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods have been found.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of Odisha.
• The site is expected to become a major archaeological and tourist attraction, bolstering cultural tourism in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: ASI.
Discovery: Ancient Buddhist monastery remains.
Location: Odisha, near Udayagiri-Ratnagiri-Lalitgiri complex.
Artifacts: Stupas, Buddha statues, terracotta seals, pottery (Gupta/post-Gupta).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the discovery of ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Odisha for understanding the historical spread and development of Buddhism in Eastern India.
Analyze how such archaeological findings contribute to the broader narrative of India's cultural heritage and its connections with other ancient civilizations.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting newly discovered archaeological sites, particularly in terms of tourism and local development.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in exploring and protecting India's rich historical legacy.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavation site showing ancient brick structures and a Buddha statue.
GS1.trivia: The Udayagiri, Ratnagiri, and Lalitgiri hills in Odisha form the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Privacy in Digital Healthcare Records, Mandates Robust Data Protection Framework
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, affirmed that the right to privacy extends to an individual's digital healthcare data.
• The ruling mandates the government to establish a comprehensive data protection framework specifically for health records, ensuring consent-based access and strict confidentiality.
• The court emphasized the need for robust security measures to prevent data breaches and misuse of sensitive personal health information.
• This judgment comes in the context of increasing digitization of health services and the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
• It highlights the delicate balance between technological advancement in healthcare and safeguarding fundamental rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on Right to Privacy regarding digital healthcare data.
Mandates government to establish data protection framework for health records.
Emphasizes consent-based access and confidentiality.
Context: Digitization of health services, Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the ongoing digitization of healthcare services in India, particularly for initiatives like the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust data protection framework for sensitive health information, balancing innovation with individual rights.
Examine the constitutional basis for the right to privacy in the digital age and its evolving interpretation by the judiciary.
Suggest measures the government can take to ensure compliance with the ruling and build public trust in digital health platforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel on a legal document with a digital medical icon in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Enhanced Maritime Security and Trade Facilitation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing deeper cooperation in maritime security, trade, and digital connectivity.
• Leaders agreed on a roadmap to counter traditional and non-traditional maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.
• Discussions also focused on expediting negotiations for the review of the ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) to boost bilateral trade.
• India reiterated its commitment to ASEAN's centrality in the Indo-Pacific region and the importance of a rules-based international order.
• Digital transformation and green economy initiatives were identified as new pillars for future collaboration.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Focus areas of India-ASEAN Summit: Maritime security, trade, digital connectivity.
Agreement to counter piracy, illegal fishing, drug trafficking.
Review of ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA).
India's stance on ASEAN centrality in Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of enhanced maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss how the proposed review of AITIGA can benefit India's economic ties with Southeast Asian nations and contribute to regional economic integration.
Examine India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in strengthening relations with ASEAN, highlighting challenges and opportunities.
Evaluate the potential for India-ASEAN collaboration in emerging areas like digital transformation and green economy initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 by Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Launches 'POSHAN 2.0' Initiative to Combat Malnutrition with Integrated Approach
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) officially launched 'POSHAN 2.0', an upgraded and integrated national nutrition mission.
• The initiative aims to address malnutrition among children, adolescent girls, pregnant women, and lactating mothers through a multi-sectoral approach.
• Key features include enhanced focus on early childhood care, promotion of traditional food systems, and leveraging technology for monitoring.
• It integrates the erstwhile Supplementary Nutrition Programme and the POSHAN Abhiyaan, streamlining efforts for greater impact.
• POSHAN 2.0 emphasizes community participation and behavioral change communication to achieve its targets.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Initiative: POSHAN 2.0.
Launched by: National Health Mission (NHM).
Target beneficiaries: Children, adolescent girls, pregnant & lactating mothers.
Approach: Multi-sectoral, integrates previous schemes, leverages technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and objectives of POSHAN 2.0 and how it seeks to improve upon its predecessor, POSHAN Abhiyaan, in addressing malnutrition in India.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach adopted by POSHAN 2.0 and its potential effectiveness in tackling the complex issue of malnutrition.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in the successful implementation of such large-scale nutrition programs.
Critically evaluate the challenges that POSHAN 2.0 might face in achieving its targets and suggest measures to overcome them.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic showing healthy children and diverse food, with POSHAN 2.0 logo.
GS2.trivia: The original POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government schemes for health and nutrition

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Political Stability and Strengthen Democracy
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution (Anti-Defection Law).
• Key recommendations include limiting the Speaker's discretionary powers in defection cases and setting a strict timeline for decisions.
• The committee also suggested clarifying what constitutes 'voluntary giving up of membership' and 'defiance of party whip'.
• The aim is to curb frequent defections, enhance political stability, and preserve the integrity of the democratic process.
• The report advocates for a clearer definition of 'split' and 'merger' to prevent misuse of the law.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Recommendations by: Parliamentary Standing Committee.
Subject: Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law).
Key recommendations: Limit Speaker's power, strict timeline, clarify 'voluntary giving up' and 'defiance of whip'.
Objective: Curb defections, enhance stability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and objectives of the Anti-Defection Law and identify its shortcomings that necessitate reforms.
Analyze the specific recommendations made by the parliamentary committee, particularly regarding the Speaker's role and the definition of defection events.
Examine how these proposed reforms could impact political stability, party discipline, and the functioning of parliamentary democracy in India.
Critically evaluate the balance between ensuring party loyalty and protecting the freedom of expression of individual legislators under the anti-defection framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a legal document overlay.
GS2.trivia: The Anti-Defection Law was added to the Constitution in 1985 by the 52nd Amendment Act.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Progress, Focus on Services and Digital Trade
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have shown significant progress in key areas, including services and digital trade.
• Both sides expressed optimism about reaching a mutually beneficial agreement that could unlock substantial economic opportunities.
• Key sticking points remain on issues like market access for certain agricultural products and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, reduce tariffs, and harmonize regulatory standards.
• A strong India-EU trade pact is seen as crucial for diversifying global supply chains and strengthening strategic autonomy for both partners.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Negotiations for: India-EU FTA.
Progress in: Services and digital trade.
Sticking points: Agricultural market access, IPR.
Objectives: Boost trade, reduce tariffs, harmonize standards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy, particularly its services and manufacturing sectors, from an FTA with the EU.
Examine the key areas of contention in the negotiations, such as agricultural market access and intellectual property rights, and how they can be resolved.
Evaluate the broader geopolitical implications of a strong India-EU trade partnership in the context of global supply chain diversification and multilateral trade dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwining.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy Implementation: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Higher Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced new initiatives under the National Education Policy (NEP) to integrate vocational training into higher education.
• The focus is on making graduates industry-ready by offering skill-based courses, apprenticeships, and certifications alongside traditional degrees.
• This move aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce and enhance the employability of Indian youth.
• Collaborations with industries and Sector Skill Councils are being formalized to design relevant curricula and provide practical exposure.
• The policy emphasizes a multidisciplinary approach, allowing students to combine academic learning with vocational skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Initiative under: National Education Policy (NEP).
Focus: Vocational training in higher education.
Methods: Skill-based courses, apprenticeships, industry collaboration.
Objectives: Bridge skill gap, enhance employability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind integrating vocational training into higher education as envisioned by the National Education Policy 2020, and its potential impact on India's workforce.
Analyze how such reforms can address the skill gap and unemployment challenges among Indian youth, particularly in a rapidly evolving job market.
Examine the role of industry-academia collaboration and Sector Skill Councils in successfully implementing vocational education programs.
Critically evaluate the challenges in transitioning from a traditional academic-focused system to a more multidisciplinary and skill-oriented higher education framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a vocational training workshop, learning practical skills.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, making it the first new education policy in 34 years.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh' to Bolster Agricultural Resilience and Farmer Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh', a new fund aimed at strengthening the agricultural sector.
• The Kosh will focus on providing financial support for climate-resilient agriculture, modern irrigation techniques, and post-harvest management.
• It seeks to enhance farmers' income by promoting value addition, market linkages, and reducing agricultural waste.
• Special emphasis will be given to small and marginal farmers, with provisions for easier access to credit and technology.
• The initiative aligns with the government's broader vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and doubling farmers' income.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage
Transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Name of the new fund: Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh.
Key objectives: Climate-resilient agriculture, modern irrigation, post-harvest management, farmer income enhancement.
Beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers.
Alignment with: Atmanirbhar Bharat, Doubling Farmers' Income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Atmanirbhar Krishi Kosh in addressing contemporary challenges faced by the Indian agricultural sector, such as climate change impacts and market inefficiencies.
Analyze how the fund's focus on technology and market linkages can contribute to the long-term sustainability and profitability of farming.
Critically evaluate the potential impact on small and marginal farmers and the broader rural economy.
Examine the role of such targeted funds in achieving the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in the agricultural domain.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a farmer in a lush field with modern farming equipment and a government scheme logo.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, cotton, and groundnuts.

GS3.headline: New National Green Hydrogen Mission Guidelines Released, Focus on Production Incentives and R&D
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released detailed guidelines for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, outlining implementation strategies.
• The guidelines include a scheme for incentivizing green hydrogen production and promoting domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• A significant portion of the mission's outlay is earmarked for research and development (R&D) in cost-effective green hydrogen technologies.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing dependence on fossil fuels.
• It also emphasizes skill development and creating a robust regulatory framework for the burgeoning green hydrogen ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
Key components: Production incentives, electrolyzer manufacturing, R&D.
Objective: India as a global green hydrogen hub, fossil fuel reduction.
Other focus areas: Skill development, regulatory framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the economic implications of incentivizing green hydrogen production and domestic electrolyzer manufacturing on India's industrial sector.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into the existing energy infrastructure.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen as a key driver for decarbonization across various sectors of the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Conceptual image of green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity through a process called electrolysis.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype, Paving Way for Cost-Effective Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of the vehicle from space, demonstrating autonomous navigation and precision landing capabilities.
• This achievement is a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space mission costs.
• The RLV technology is critical for future human spaceflight missions (Gaganyaan) and deploying larger satellite constellations.
• ISRO aims to achieve space transportation at a fraction of current costs, enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's successful test: Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
Nature of test: Controlled landing experiment.
Objective: Reduce space mission costs, develop two-stage orbital vehicle.
Significance: Future human spaceflight (Gaganyaan), satellite deployment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its aspirations in the global space market.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable for various applications, including scientific research and commercial launches.
Examine the technical challenges involved in developing and perfecting RLV technology, drawing parallels with global efforts in this domain.
Evaluate the potential impact of this achievement on India's human spaceflight mission (Gaganyaan) and its long-term space exploration goals.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) descending for a landing.
GS3.trivia: The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is a winged body aerospace vehicle operating in hypersonic flight regime.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism Amid Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI noted a moderation in core inflation but highlighted risks from volatile food prices and geopolitical tensions.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while remaining agile in its policy stance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is likely to maintain a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance for the foreseeable future.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5%.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision.
Reason: Balance inflation with growth, global uncertainties.
Stance: 'Withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst evolving domestic and global economic conditions.
Analyze the implications of the RBI's monetary policy stance on inflation, economic growth, and the financial sector in India.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation, particularly considering factors like food price volatility and global supply chain disruptions.
Critically evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its potential impact on credit growth and investment.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Border Security Forces Enhance Surveillance Along Western Border with AI-Powered Drones and Integrated Command System
GS3.explainer:
• India's Border Security Forces (BSF) have significantly upgraded their surveillance capabilities along the western border.
• The deployment includes advanced AI-powered drones equipped with thermal imaging and facial recognition technology.
• These drones are integrated into a centralized command and control system, enabling real-time threat assessment and rapid response.
• The initiative aims to counter infiltration attempts, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism more effectively.
• This technological leap is part of a broader strategy to modernize India's border management and enhance national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: BSF's new technology: AI-powered drones.
Location: Western border.
Features: Thermal imaging, facial recognition, integrated command system.
Purpose: Counter infiltration, smuggling, terrorism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of technology, specifically AI-powered drones, in modernizing border management and enhancing India's internal security architecture.
Analyze the advantages and challenges associated with deploying advanced surveillance technologies in sensitive border areas.
Examine how such technological advancements can deter cross-border infiltration and organized crime, while also considering ethical implications.
Suggest other measures that can complement technological solutions for comprehensive border security management in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Drone flying over a desert landscape near a border fence at sunset.
GS3.trivia: The Border Security Force (BSF) is India's primary border guarding organization on its border with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of technology in border security

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Discover Novel Enzyme for Plastic Degradation, Offering Hope for Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) have identified a novel enzyme capable of efficiently degrading polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic.
• This enzyme, discovered in a local bacterial strain, shows promising results in breaking down plastic at ambient temperatures, unlike many existing solutions.
• The discovery holds significant potential for developing scalable and eco-friendly methods for plastic waste management and recycling.
• Further research is underway to optimize the enzyme's activity and explore its application in industrial settings.
• This breakthrough could contribute significantly to addressing India's growing plastic pollution crisis.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Environmental pollution and degradation
Bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Discovery by: Indian Institute of Science (IISc) researchers.
Discovery: Novel enzyme for PET plastic degradation.
Key feature: Efficient at ambient temperatures.
Potential: Scalable, eco-friendly plastic recycling.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of discovering a novel enzyme for plastic degradation in addressing the global plastic pollution crisis, particularly in the Indian context.
Analyze the advantages of enzymatic degradation methods over traditional plastic recycling techniques in terms of environmental impact and efficiency.
Examine the challenges involved in scaling up such biotechnological solutions for industrial application and their potential economic viability.
Suggest how this scientific breakthrough can be integrated into India's broader waste management strategies and contribute to a circular economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Microscope image of bacteria on plastic, with an enzyme model overlay.
GS3.trivia: PET plastic is commonly used for beverage bottles and food containers.

GS3.headline: India Crosses 100 GW Mark in Installed Solar Power Capacity, Boosting Renewable Energy Targets
GS3.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone by crossing 100 Gigawatts (GW) of installed solar power capacity.
• This includes both large-scale solar parks and rooftop solar installations across the country.
• The achievement underscores India's rapid progress in the renewable energy sector and its commitment to climate goals.
• This milestone significantly contributes to India's target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Government policies, subsidies, and private sector investments have been instrumental in driving this growth.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Milestone: 100 GW installed solar power capacity.
Includes: Solar parks and rooftop solar.
Contribution to: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy target by 2030.
